contents: From HoTT Require Import Basics Types.
From HoTT Require Import Basics Types.

(** * BAut(X) *)

(** ** Basics *)

(** [BAut X] is the type of types that are merely equal to [X]. It is connected, by [is0connected_component] and any two points are merely equal by [merely_path_component]. *)
Definition BAut (X : Type@{u}) := { Z : Type@{u} & merely (Z = X) }.
Coercion BAut_pr1 X : BAut X -> Type := pr1.
Instance ispointed_baut {X : Type} : IsPointed (BAut X) := (X; tr 1).
(** We also define a pointed version [pBAut X], since the coercion [BAut_pr1] doesn't work if [BAut X] is a [pType]. *)

Definition path_baut `{Univalence} {X} (Z Z' : BAut X)
: (Z <~> Z') <~> (Z = Z' :> BAut X)
  := equiv_path_sigma_hprop _ _ oE equiv_path_universe _ _.

goals.conclusion: ap (BAut_pr1 X) (path_baut Z Z' f) = path_universe f
ap (BAut_pr1 X) (path_baut Z Z' f) = path_universe f
ap pr1 (path_sigma_hprop Z Z' (path_universe_uncurried f)) = path_universe f
transport (fun W : BAut X => W) (path_baut Z Z' f) z = f z
transport (fun W : BAut X => W) (path_baut Z Z' f) z = f z
transport idmap (ap (BAut_pr1 X) (path_baut Z Z' f)) z = f z

transport idmap (ap (BAut_pr1 X) (path_baut Z Z' f)) z =
transport idmap (path_universe f) z

contents: (** The following tactic, which applies when trying to prove an hprop, replaces all assumed elements of [BAut X] by [X] itself. With [Univalence], this would work for any 0-connected type, but using [merely_path_component] we can avoid univalence. *)

(** ** Truncation *)

(** If [X] is an [n.+1]-type, then [BAut X] is an [n.+2]-type. *)

Instance trunc_baut `{Univalence} {n X} `{IsTrunc n.+1 X}
: IsTrunc n.+2 (BAut X).

goals.conclusion: IsTrunc n.+2 (BAut X)
IsTrunc n.+2 (BAut X)
forall x y : BAut X, IsTrunc n.+1 (x = y)
IsTrunc n.+1 (Z = W)
IsTrunc n.+1 (pt = pt)

contents: (** If [X] is truncated, then so is every element of [BAut X]. *)

Instance trunc_el_baut {n X} `{Funext} `{IsTrunc n X} (Z : BAut X)
  : IsTrunc n Z
  := ltac:(by baut_reduce).

(** ** Operations on [BAut] *)

(** Multiplying by a fixed type *)

Definition baut_prod_r (X A : Type)
  : BAut X -> BAut (X * A)
  := fun Z:BAut X =>
       (Z * A ; Trunc_functor (-1) (ap (fun W => W * A)) (pr2 Z))
       : BAut (X * A).

Definition ap_baut_prod_r `{Univalence} (X A : Type)
           {Z W : BAut X} (e : Z <~> W)
  : ap (baut_prod_r X A) (path_baut Z W e)
    = path_baut (baut_prod_r X A Z) (baut_prod_r X A W) (equiv_functor_prod_r e).

goals.conclusion:
ap (baut_prod_r X A) (path_baut Z W e) =
path_baut (baut_prod_r X A Z) (baut_prod_r X A W) (equiv_functor_prod_r e)

ap (baut_prod_r X A) (path_baut Z W e) =
path_baut (baut_prod_r X A Z) (baut_prod_r X A W) (equiv_functor_prod_r e)

ap (baut_prod_r X A) (path_sigma_hprop Z W (path_universe_uncurried e)) =
path_sigma_hprop (baut_prod_r X A Z) (baut_prod_r X A W)
  (path_universe_uncurried (equiv_functor_prod_r e))

ap pr1
  (ap (baut_prod_r X A) (path_sigma_hprop Z W (path_universe_uncurried e))) =
path_universe_uncurried (equiv_functor_prod_r e)

contents: rewrite <- (ap_compose (baut_prod_r X A) pr1 (path_sigma_hprop Z W _)).

goals.conclusion:
ap (fun x : BAut X => (baut_prod_r X A x).1)
  (path_sigma_hprop Z W (path_universe_uncurried e)) =
path_universe_uncurried (equiv_functor_prod_r e)

contents: rewrite <- ((ap_compose pr1 (fun Z => Z * A) (path_sigma_hprop Z W _))^).

goals.conclusion:
ap (fun Z0 : Type => Z0 * A)
  (ap pr1 (path_sigma_hprop Z W (path_universe_uncurried e))) =
path_universe_uncurried (equiv_functor_prod_r e)

ap (fun Z0 : Type => Z0 * A) (path_universe_uncurried e) =
path_universe_uncurried (equiv_functor_prod_r e)

equiv_path (Z * A) (W * A)
  (ap (fun Z0 : Type => Z0 * A) (path_universe_uncurried e)) =
equiv_functor_prod_r e

contents:
(** ** Centers *)

(** The following lemma says that to define a section of a family [P] of hsets over [BAut X], it is equivalent to define an element of [P X] which is fixed by all automorphisms of [X]. *)

goals.conclusion:
{e : P pt & forall g : X <~> X, transport P (path_universe g) e = e} <~>
(forall Z : BAut X, P Z)

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
{e : P pt & forall g : X <~> X, transport P (path_universe g) e = e} <~>
(forall Z : BAut X, P Z)

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
{e : P pt & forall g : X <~> X, transport P (path_universe g) e = e} <~>
(forall (x : Type) (y : merely (x = X)), P (x; y))

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

contents: (** We use the fact that maps out of a propositional truncation into an hset are equivalent to weakly constant functions. *)

refine ((equiv_functor_forall'
             (P := fun Z => { f : (Z=X) -> P Z & WeaklyConstant f })
             1
             (fun Z => equiv_merely_rec_hset_if_domain _ _)) oE _); simpl.

goals.conclusion: Z = X -> IsHSet (P Z)

hypotheses.type: forall Z0 : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z0)

goals.conclusion:
{e : P X & forall g : X <~> X, transport P (path_universe g) e = e} <~>
(forall a : Type, {f : a = X -> P a & WeaklyConstant f})

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion: Z = X -> IsHSet (P Z)

hypotheses.type: forall Z0 : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z0)

goals.conclusion: IsHSet (P Z)

hypotheses.type: forall Z0 : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z0)

goals.conclusion: IsHSet (P (Z; tr p))

hypotheses.type: forall Z0 : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z0)

goals.conclusion:
{e : P X & forall g : X <~> X, transport P (path_universe g) e = e} <~>
(forall a : Type, {f : a = X -> P a & WeaklyConstant f})

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
{e : P X & forall g : X <~> X, transport P (path_universe g) e = e} <~>
(forall a : Type, {f : a = X -> P a & forall x y : a = X, f x = f y})

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

contents: (** Now we peel away a bunch of contractible types. *)

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion: P X <~> (forall x : Type, x = X -> P x)

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
forall a : P X,
(fun e : P X => forall g : X <~> X, transport P (path_universe g) e = e) a <~>
(fun f : forall x : Type, x = X -> P x =>
 forall (x : Type) (x0 y : x = X), f x x0 = f x y) 
  (?f a)

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
forall a : P X,
(fun e : P X => forall g : X <~> X, transport P (path_universe g) e = e) a <~>
(fun f : forall x : Type, x = X -> P x =>
 forall (x : Type) (x0 y : x = X), f x x0 = f x y)
  (equiv_paths_ind_r X (fun (x : Type) (_ : x = X) => P x) a)

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
(forall g : X <~> X, transport P (path_universe g) p = p) <~>
(forall (x : Type) (x0 y : x = X),
 paths_ind_r X (fun (x1 : Type) (_ : x1 = X) => P x1) p x x0 =
 paths_ind_r X (fun (x1 : Type) (_ : x1 = X) => P x1) p x y)

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
(forall g : X <~> X, transport P (path_universe g) p = p) <~>
(forall y : X = X,
 paths_ind_r X (fun (x : Type) (_ : x = X) => P x) p X 1 =
 paths_ind_r X (fun (x : Type) (_ : x = X) => P x) p X y)

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)
forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
forall b : X = X,
(fun a : X <~> X => transport P (path_universe a) p = p) (?f b) <~>
(fun b0 : X = X =>
 paths_ind_r X (fun (x : Type) (_ : x = X) => P x) p X 1 =
 paths_ind_r X (fun (x : Type) (_ : x = X) => P x) p X b0) b

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
forall b : X = X,
(fun a : X <~> X => transport P (path_universe a) p = p)
  (equiv_equiv_path X X b) <~>
(fun b0 : X = X =>
 paths_ind_r X (fun (x : Type) (_ : x = X) => P x) p X 1 =
 paths_ind_r X (fun (x : Type) (_ : x = X) => P x) p X b0) b

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
transport P (path_universe (transport idmap e)) p = p <~>
p = paths_ind_r X (fun (x : Type) (_ : x = X) => P x) p X e

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
p = transport P (path_universe (transport idmap e))^ p <~>
p = paths_ind_r X (fun (x : Type) (_ : x = X) => P x) p X e

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion:
transport P (path_universe (transport idmap e))^ p =
paths_ind_r X (fun (x : Type) (_ : x = X) => P x) p X e

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

goals.conclusion: transport P e^ p = transport P e^ p

hypotheses.type: forall Z : BAut X, IsHSet (P Z)

contents: (** This implies that if [X] is a set, then the center of [BAut X] is the set of automorphisms of [X] that commute with every other automorphism (i.e. the center, in the usual sense, of the group of automorphisms of [X]). *)

goals.conclusion:
{f : X <~> X & forall g : X <~> X, g o f == f o g} <~>
(forall Z : BAut X, Z = Z)

{f : X <~> X & forall g : X <~> X, g o f == f o g} <~>
(forall Z : BAut X, Z = Z)

contents:
refine (equiv_functor_forall_id
            (fun Z => equiv_path_sigma_hprop Z Z) oE _).

goals.conclusion:
{f : X <~> X &
forall g : X <~> X, (fun x : X => g (f x)) == (fun x : X => f (g x))} <~>
(forall a : {Z : Type & merely (Z = X)}, a.1 = a.1)

contents: refine (baut_ind_hset X (fun Z => Z = Z) oE _).

goals.conclusion:
{f : X <~> X &
forall g : X <~> X, (fun x : X => g (f x)) == (fun x : X => f (g x))} <~>
{e : pt = pt &
forall g : X <~> X, transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe g) e = e}

{f : X <~> X &
forall g : X <~> X, (fun x : X => g (f x)) == (fun x : X => f (g x))} <~>
{e : X = X &
forall g : X <~> X, transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe g) e = e}

(forall g : X <~> X, (fun x : X => g (f x)) == (fun x : X => f (g x))) <~>
(forall g : X <~> X,
 transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe g)
   (equiv_path_universe X X f) =
 equiv_path_universe X X f)

(fun x : X => g (f x)) == (fun x : X => f (g x)) <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe g)
  (path_universe_uncurried f) =
path_universe_uncurried f

(fun x : X => g (f x)) = (fun x : X => f (g x)) <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe g)
  (path_universe_uncurried f) =
path_universe_uncurried f

contents: refine (_ oE equiv_path_equiv (g oE f) (f oE g)).

goals.conclusion:
g oE f = f oE g <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe g)
  (path_universe_uncurried f) =
path_universe_uncurried f

forall g : X <~> X,
g oE f = f oE g <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe g)
  (path_universe_uncurried f) =
path_universe_uncurried f

equiv_path X X g oE f = f oE equiv_path X X g <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe (equiv_path X X g))
  (path_universe_uncurried f) =
path_universe_uncurried f

forall f : X <~> X,
equiv_path X X g oE f = f oE equiv_path X X g <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe (equiv_path X X g))
  (path_universe_uncurried f) =
path_universe_uncurried f

equiv_path X X g oE equiv_path X X f = equiv_path X X f oE equiv_path X X g <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe (equiv_path X X g))
  (path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)) =
path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)

equiv_path X X (f @ g) = equiv_path X X f oE equiv_path X X g <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe (equiv_path X X g))
  (path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)) =
path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)

equiv_path X X (f @ g) = equiv_path X X (g @ f) <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe (equiv_path X X g))
  (path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)) =
path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)

f @ g = g @ f <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => Z = Z) (path_universe (equiv_path X X g))
  (path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)) =
path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)

f @ g = g @ f <~>
((path_universe (equiv_path X X g))^ @
 path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)) @
path_universe (equiv_path X X g) = path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)

f @ g = g @ f <~>
(path_universe (equiv_path X X g))^ @
(path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f) @
 path_universe (equiv_path X X g)) =
path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)

f @ g = g @ f <~>
path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f) @ path_universe (equiv_path X X g) =
path_universe (equiv_path X X g) @ path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)

path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f) @ path_universe (equiv_path X X g) =
f @ g

g @ f =
path_universe (equiv_path X X g) @ path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)

path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f) @ path_universe (equiv_path X X g) =
f @ g

g @ f =
path_universe (equiv_path X X g) @ path_universe_uncurried (equiv_path X X f)

contents: (** We show that this equivalence takes the identity equivalence to the identity in the center.  We have to be careful in this proof never to [simpl] or [unfold] too many things, or Coq will produce gigantic terms that take it forever to compute with. *)

Definition id_center_baut `{Univalence} X `{IsHSet X}
: center_baut X (exist
                   (fun (f:X<~>X) => forall (g:X<~>X), g o f == f o g)
                   (equiv_idmap X)
                   (fun (g:X<~>X) (x:X) => idpath (g x)))
  = fun Z => idpath Z.

goals.conclusion:
center_baut X (1%equiv; fun (g : X <~> X) (x : X) => 1) =
(fun Z : BAut X => 1)

center_baut X (1%equiv; fun (g : X <~> X) (x : X) => 1) =
(fun Z : BAut X => 1)
center_baut X (1%equiv; fun (g : X <~> X) (x : X) => 1) Z = 1
center_baut X (1%equiv; fun (g : X <~> X) (x : X) => 1) Z = 1
center_baut X (1%equiv; fun (g : X <~> X) (x : X) => 1) pt = 1

contents:
(** Similarly, if [X] is a 1-type, we can characterize the 2-center of [BAut X]. *)

(** Coq is too eager about unfolding some things appearing in this proof. *)

goals.conclusion:
{f : forall x : X, x = x &
forall (g : X <~> X) (x : X), ap g (f x) = f (g x)} <~>
(forall Z : BAut X, 1 = 1)

{f : forall x : X, x = x &
forall (g : X <~> X) (x : X), ap g (f x) = f (g x)} <~>
(forall Z : BAut X, 1 = 1)

contents:
refine ((equiv_functor_forall_id
               (fun Z => (equiv_concat_lr _ _)
                           oE (equiv_ap (equiv_path_sigma_hprop Z Z) 1%path 1%path))) oE _).

goals.conclusion: 1 = equiv_path_sigma_hprop Z Z 1
equiv_path_sigma_hprop Z Z 1 = 1

{f : forall x : X, x = x &
forall (g : X <~> X) (x : X), ap g (f x) = f (g x)} <~>
(forall a : {Z : Type & merely (Z = X)}, 1 = 1)
1 = equiv_path_sigma_hprop Z Z 1
equiv_path_sigma_hprop Z Z 1 = 1

{f : forall x : X, x = x &
forall (g : X <~> X) (x : X), ap g (f x) = f (g x)} <~>
(forall a : {Z : Type & merely (Z = X)}, 1 = 1)
equiv_path_sigma_hprop Z Z 1 = 1

{f : forall x : X, x = x &
forall (g : X <~> X) (x : X), ap g (f x) = f (g x)} <~>
(forall a : {Z : Type & merely (Z = X)}, 1 = 1)

contents: assert (forall Z:BAut X, IsHSet (idpath Z.1 = idpath Z.1)) by exact _.

{f : forall x : X, x = x &
forall (g : X <~> X) (x : X), ap g (f x) = f (g x)} <~>
(forall a : {Z : Type & merely (Z = X)}, 1 = 1)

contents: refine (baut_ind_hset X (fun Z => idpath Z = idpath Z) oE _).

goals.conclusion:
{f : forall x : X, x = x &
forall (g : X <~> X) (x : X), ap g (f x) = f (g x)} <~>
{e : 1 = 1 &
forall g : X <~> X, transport (fun Z : Type => 1 = 1) (path_universe g) e = e}
(forall x : X, x = x) <~> 1 = 1

forall a : forall x : X, x = x,
(fun f : forall x : X, x = x =>
 forall (g : X <~> X) (x : X), ap g (f x) = f (g x)) a <~>
(fun e : 1 = 1 =>
 forall g : X <~> X,
 transport (fun Z : Type => 1 = 1) (path_universe g) e = e) 
  (?f a)
(forall x : X, x = x) <~> 1 = 1
path_universe 1%equiv = path_universe 1%equiv <~> 1 = 1
1 = path_universe 1%equiv
path_universe 1%equiv = 1
1 = path_universe 1%equiv
path_universe 1%equiv = 1

forall a : forall x : X, x = x,
(fun f : forall x : X, x = x =>
 forall (g : X <~> X) (x : X), ap g (f x) = f (g x)) a <~>
(fun e : 1 = 1 =>
 forall g : X <~> X,
 transport (fun Z : Type => 1 = 1) (path_universe g) e = e)
  ((equiv_concat_lr path_universe_1^ path_universe_1
    oE equiv_path2_universe 1 1) a)

(fun f0 : forall x : X, x = x =>
 forall (g : X <~> X) (x : X), ap g (f0 x) = f0 (g x)) f <~>
(fun e : 1 = 1 =>
 forall g : X <~> X,
 transport (fun Z : Type => 1 = 1) (path_universe g) e = e)
  ((equiv_concat_lr path_universe_1^ path_universe_1
    oE equiv_path2_universe 1 1) f)

(forall x : X, ap g (f x) = f (g x)) <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => 1 = 1) (path_universe g)
  ((equiv_concat_lr path_universe_1^ path_universe_1
    oE equiv_path2_universe 1 1) f) =
(equiv_concat_lr path_universe_1^ path_universe_1 oE equiv_path2_universe 1 1)
  f

path2_universe (fun x : X => ap g (f x)) =
path2_universe (fun x : X => f (g x)) <~>
transport (fun Z : Type => 1 = 1) (path_universe g)
  ((equiv_concat_lr path_universe_1^ path_universe_1
    oE equiv_path2_universe 1 1) f) =
(equiv_concat_lr path_universe_1^ path_universe_1 oE equiv_path2_universe 1 1)
  f

path2_universe (fun x : X => ap g (f x)) =
path2_universe (fun x : X => f (g x)) <~>
((concat_1p (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerR
   ((equiv_concat_lr path_universe_1^ path_universe_1
     oE equiv_path2_universe 1 1) f)
   (path_universe g)) @
concat_1p (path_universe g) =
((concat_p1 (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerL (path_universe g)
   ((equiv_concat_lr path_universe_1^ path_universe_1
     oE equiv_path2_universe 1 1) f)) @
concat_p1 (path_universe g)

path2_universe (fun x : X => ap g (f x)) =
path2_universe (fun x : X => f (g x)) <~>
((concat_1p (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerR ((path_universe_1^ @ equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f) @ path_universe_1)
   (path_universe g)) @
concat_1p (path_universe g) =
((concat_p1 (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerL (path_universe g)
   ((path_universe_1^ @ equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f) @ path_universe_1)) @
concat_p1 (path_universe g)

contents: change (equiv_idmap X == equiv_idmap X) in f.

goals.conclusion:
path2_universe (fun x : X => ap g (f x)) =
path2_universe (fun x : X => f (g x)) <~>
((concat_1p (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerR ((path_universe_1^ @ equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f) @ path_universe_1)
   (path_universe g)) @
concat_1p (path_universe g) =
((concat_p1 (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerL (path_universe g)
   ((path_universe_1^ @ equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f) @ path_universe_1)) @
concat_p1 (path_universe g)

((concat_1p (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerR ((path_universe_1^ @ equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f) @ path_universe_1)
   (path_universe g)) @
concat_1p (path_universe g) = path2_universe (fun x : X => ap g (f x))

path2_universe (fun x : X => f (g x)) =
((concat_p1 (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerL (path_universe g)
   ((path_universe_1^ @ equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f) @ path_universe_1)) @
concat_p1 (path_universe g)

((concat_1p (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerR ((path_universe_1^ @ equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f) @ path_universe_1)
   (path_universe g)) @
concat_1p (path_universe g) = path2_universe (fun x : X => ap g (f x))

((concat_1p (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerR ((path_universe_1^ @ equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f) @ path_universe_1)
   (path_universe g)) @
concat_1p (path_universe g) =
((((concat_1p (path_universe g))^ @
   whiskerR path_universe_1^ (path_universe g)) @
  whiskerR (equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f) (path_universe g)) @
 whiskerR path_universe_1 (path_universe g)) @
concat_1p (path_universe g)

path2_universe (fun x : X => f (g x)) =
((concat_p1 (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerL (path_universe g)
   ((path_universe_1^ @ equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f) @ path_universe_1)) @
concat_p1 (path_universe g)

((((concat_p1 (path_universe g))^ @
   whiskerL (path_universe g) path_universe_1^) @
  whiskerL (path_universe g) (equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f)) @
 whiskerL (path_universe g) path_universe_1) @
concat_p1 (path_universe g) =
((concat_p1 (path_universe g))^ @
 whiskerL (path_universe g)
   ((path_universe_1^ @ equiv_path2_universe 1 1 f) @ path_universe_1)) @
concat_p1 (path_universe g)

contents: (** Once again we compute it on the identity.  In this case it seems to be unavoidable to do some [simpl]ing (or at least [cbn]ing), making this proof somewhat slower. *)

Definition id_center2_baut `{Univalence} X `{IsTrunc 1 X}
  : center2_baut X (exist
                      (fun (f:forall x:X, x=x) =>
                         forall (g:X<~>X) (x:X), ap g (f x) = f (g x))
                      (fun x => idpath x)
                      (fun (g:X<~>X) (x:X) => idpath (idpath (g x))))
    = fun Z => idpath (idpath Z).

goals.conclusion:
center2_baut X (fun x : X => 1; fun (g : X <~> X) (x : X) => 1) =
(fun Z : BAut X => 1)

center2_baut X (fun x : X => 1; fun (g : X <~> X) (x : X) => 1) =
(fun Z : BAut X => 1)
center2_baut X (fun x : X => 1; fun (g : X <~> X) (x : X) => 1) Z = 1

contents: assert (IsHSet (idpath Z.1 = idpath Z.1)) by exact _.

goals.conclusion: center2_baut X (fun x : X => 1; fun (g : X <~> X) (x : X) => 1) Z = 1
center2_baut X (fun x : X => 1; fun (g : X <~> X) (x : X) => 1) pt = 1

((path_sigma_hprop_1 pt)^ @
 ap (path_sigma_hprop pt pt)
   ((path_universe_1^ @ equiv_path2_universe 1 1 (fun x : X => 1)) @
    path_universe_1)) @
path_sigma_hprop_1 pt = 1

((path_sigma_hprop_1 pt)^ @
 ap (path_sigma_hprop pt pt) ((path_universe_1^ @ 1) @ path_universe_1)) @
path_sigma_hprop_1 pt = 1

((path_sigma_hprop_1 pt)^ @ ap (path_sigma_hprop pt pt) 1) @
path_sigma_hprop_1 pt = 1
((path_sigma_hprop_1 pt)^ @ 1) @ path_sigma_hprop_1 pt = 1

contents:
(** ** Maps into [BAut F] classify bundles with fiber [F] *)

  (** The property of being merely equivalent to a given type [F] defines a subuniverse. *)

goals.conclusion: Subuniverse
Subuniverse

forall T U : Type,
merely (T <~> F) -> forall f : T -> U, IsEquiv f -> merely (U <~> F)

contents: intros T U mere_eq f iseq_f.

goals.conclusion: merely (U <~> F)
merely (U <~> F)
merely (U <~> F)

contents: (** The universe of O-local types for [subuniverse_merely_equiv F] is equivalent to [BAut F]. *)

Proposition equiv_baut_typeO `{Univalence} {F : Type}
    :  BAut F <~> Type_ (subuniverse_merely_equiv F).

goals.conclusion: BAut F <~> Type_ (subuniverse_merely_equiv F)
BAut F <~> Type_ (subuniverse_merely_equiv F)
Trunc (-1) (X = F) <~> Trunc (-1) (X <~> F)

contents: (** Consequently, maps into [BAut F] correspond to bundles with fibers merely equivalent to [F]. *)

Corollary equiv_map_baut_fibration `{Univalence} {Y : pType} {F : Type}
    : (Y -> BAut F) <~> { p : Slice Y & forall y:Y, merely (hfiber p.2 y <~> F) }.

goals.conclusion: (Y -> BAut F) <~> {p : Slice Y & forall y : Y, merely (hfiber p.2 y <~> F)}
(Y -> BAut F) <~> {p : Slice Y & forall y : Y, merely (hfiber p.2 y <~> F)}

(Y -> Type_ (subuniverse_merely_equiv F)) <~>
{p : Slice Y & forall y : Y, merely (hfiber p.2 y <~> F)}

{p : {X : Type & X -> Y} & MapIn (subuniverse_merely_equiv F) p.2} <~>
{p : Slice Y & forall y : Y, merely (hfiber p.2 y <~> F)}

(fun p0 : {X : Type & X -> Y} => MapIn (subuniverse_merely_equiv F) p0.2) p <~>
(fun p0 : Slice Y => forall y : Y, merely (hfiber p0.2 y <~> F)) p

In (subuniverse_merely_equiv F) (hfiber p.2 y) <~>
merely (hfiber p.2 y <~> F)

contents:
Proposition pequiv_pbaut_typeOp@{u v +} `{Univalence} {F : Type@{u}}
    : pBAut@{u v} F <~>* [Type_ (subuniverse_merely_equiv F), (F; tr equiv_idmap)].

goals.conclusion: pBAut F <~>* [Type_ (subuniverse_merely_equiv F), (F; tr 1%equiv)]
pBAut F <~>* [Type_ (subuniverse_merely_equiv F), (F; tr 1%equiv)]
BAut F <~> Type_ (subuniverse_merely_equiv F)
?Goal ispointed_baut = (F; tr 1%equiv)
equiv_baut_typeO ispointed_baut = (F; tr 1%equiv)

contents: (** When [Y] is connected, pointed maps into [pBAut F] correspond to maps into the universe sending the base point to [F]. *)

goals.conclusion: (Y ->* pBAut F) <~> (Y ->* [Type, F])
(Y ->* pBAut F) <~> (Y ->* [Type, F])

(Y ->** [Type_ (subuniverse_merely_equiv F), (F; tr 1%equiv)]) <~>
(Y ->* [Type, F])

contents: (** When [Y] is connected, [pBAut F] classifies fiber sequences over [Y] with fiber [F]. *)
